title: Gated-action prompts for anonymous users
description: Render save/track/create-project/enable-alert actions as visible-but-disabled for anonymous users, prompting login/registration on interaction, with a single no-op entitlement hook point per gated action reserved for future subscription checks.
acceptance_criteria: Save/track/create-project/alert controls are visible (not hidden) to anonymous users but disabled, with a clear login/registration prompt on click
Each gated action has a single documented requires_entitlement (or equivalent) check point that currently always evaluates to 'allowed once logged in' — no billing/subscription logic is implemented
No subscription, payment, or plan-tier concept is introduced in this feature — the hook is a no-op by design
Authenticated users see the same actions fully enabled, unaffected by the gating logic added for anonymous users
notes: See docs/adr/0005-public-vs-signed-in-experience-split.md. Explicitly: 'do not implement subscriptions yet, but leave entitlement hooks where they naturally belong' — this feature is where that hook lives.
